stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
The presence of 'safetensors' indicates modern, secure model handling practices.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its application in large language model tasks.
The model is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Notion killing Skiff-influenced email app since most users use AI agents instead
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Notion is integrating AI agents to fundamentally change how users manage their inboxes.
This move signals a shift away from traditional email client functionality towards agent-driven workflows.
The development is positioned as a response to the growing adoption of AI agents replacing standard email usage patterns.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
The associated tags indicate the use of transformers and safetensors formats, pointing to a specific model architecture.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
The associated tags (transformers, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern AI/ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using its MLX engine.
The performance improvements result in faster response times and reduced memory usage for models.
The updated engine also enables models to generate higher quality responses.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, now integrates MLX for performance optimization.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon, leveraging Apple's native ML framework.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
Its core feature is connecting these apps to local AI coding agents.
The system emphasizes running all AI processes entirely on the user's local device for privacy/control.

stories.title: Anthropic says Alibaba must be punished for largest Claude cloning attack
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Anthropic alleges Alibaba engaged in a large-scale attack to clone its Claude AI model.
The alleged attack involved using 25,000 accounts to generate over 28.8 million exchanges with Claude.
The incident raises significant concerns regarding AI data security, intellectual property, and misuse of LLMs.

stories.title: When Does Combining Language Models Help? A Co-Failure Ceiling on Routing, Voting, and Mixture-of-Agents Across 67 Frontier Models
stories.author: Josef Chen
bullet_points: The paper analyzes the performance limits of multi-model LLM systems (routing, voting, etc.) designed to improve accuracy over single models.
It introduces a theoretical 'Co-Failure Ceiling' demonstrating that the accuracy gain is fundamentally capped by the rate at which all constituent models fail on the same query (beta).
The study highlights a limitation in current diagnostic methods, showing that standard error correlation (rho) cannot accurately identify this critical failure rate (beta).

stories.title: Prompt Injection in Automated Résumé Screening with Large Language Models: Single and Multi-Injection Settings
stories.author: Stefanus Jasin
bullet_points: The research investigates 'prompt injection' specifically within LLM-powered automated résumé screening systems.
Prompt injection is defined as subtle, self-promotional text designed to manipulate LLM evaluations without adding new qualifications.
Experiments demonstrate that this injection technique reliably improves applicant rankings, especially when résumé quality is similar among candidates.

stories.title: Reinforcement Learning without Ground-Truth Solutions can Improve LLMs
stories.author: Yuxiong He
bullet_points: Introduces RiVER, a framework enabling Reinforcement Learning for LLMs without relying on ground-truth solutions.
Addresses a key limitation of current RLVR methods by using deterministic execution feedback for continuous-valued supervision.
Focuses on training LLMs via score-based optimization tasks, expanding applicability to complex, open-ended problems.

stories.title: Hallucination in World Models is Predictable and Preventable
stories.author: Xiaolong Wang
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of 'hallucination' in advanced generative world models, where simulations drift from reality.
Proposes that hallucinations are concentrated in sparsely covered regions of the state-action space, suggesting a data-centric solution.
Introduces MMBench2, a large-scale dataset designed to benchmark and test visual world modeling capabilities with ground-truth data.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon devices.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes a 'local-first' approach, allowing AI to run on user hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or config files.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is introducing experimental local image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for macOS users.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Samsung will soon start charging to access its smart home API
stories.author: Dominic Preston
bullet_points: Samsung is introducing paid tiers for access to its SmartThings API starting in October.
A new $4.99 monthly plan is available specifically for non-commercial, individual developers.
The API changes may impact not only developers but also advanced smart home users.

stories.title: Apple’s most powerful Macs might be waiting until 2027 for big processor upgrades
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Apple is reportedly altering its standard Mac silicon release cadence.
The upcoming M6 chip is expected to lack Pro or Max variants.
Apple plans to accelerate features originally slated for later releases with the M7 launch next year.

stories.title: YouTube updates Shorts to make it even more like TikTok
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: YouTube is integrating more TikTok-like features into its Shorts format.
A new 'clear screen' mode is being introduced to remove on-screen icons and text from Shorts.
The 'thumbs-up' reaction button is being replaced with a 'heart' icon.

stories.title: FCC may kill $2B program that connects schools and libraries to Internet
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: The FCC is reportedly considering terminating a $2 billion program designed to provide internet access to schools and libraries.
The potential cancellation is linked to concerns raised by Carr regarding student screen time.
The article suggests Carr's involvement is being criticized as overreaching into parental roles.

stories.title: Feds deny Polestar authorization to sell cars in US from model year 2027
stories.author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
bullet_points: Federal authorities have denied Polestar authorization to sell its vehicles in the United States.
This denial applies to models starting from the 2027 model year.
The situation differs from Volvo, which has received authorization for sales in the US.

stories.title: Language-Based Digital Twins for Elderly Cognitive Assistance
stories.author: Hiroko H. Dodge
bullet_points: Proposes a language-based digital twin framework for personalized healthcare.
Leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) to model and mimic elderly conversational behavior.
Aims to use language patterns as non-invasive biomarkers for early detection of Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI).

stories.title: Empowering GUI Agents via Autonomous Experience Exploration and Hindsight Experience Utilization for Task Planning
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: The research addresses limitations in small, open-source Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) used for GUI agent task planning.
A new method, PEEU (Planning Experience Exploration and Utilization), is introduced to improve autonomous exploration and task decomposition.
The work focuses on enabling cost-efficient and privacy-preserving agents to handle complex, repetitive GUI tasks across different websites.

stories.title: AI Healthcare Chatbots as Information Infrastructure: A Large-Scale Study of User-Reported Breakdowns
stories.author: Masooda Bashir
bullet_points: The study analyzes over 15,000 user reviews across 59 AI healthcare chatbots to assess their real-world performance.
Three primary recurring breakdowns were identified: access barriers/service unreliability, user experience issues, and interaction quality problems.
The research addresses the need to study the performance and impact of AI chatbots in everyday informational and emotional health contexts.

stories.title: DanceOPD: On-Policy Generative Field Distillation
stories.author: Tat-Seng Chua
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of unifying diverse capabilities (T2I, local/global editing) in modern image generation models.
Identifies a core conflict where editing often degrades text-to-image performance and where global/local edits interfere.
Introduces 'DanceOPD' as a new method to effectively compose and align these conflicting image generation capabilities.

stories.title: When are likely answers right? On Sequence Probability and Correctness in LLMs
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: The paper investigates the fundamental relationship between sequence probability (as predicted by LLMs) and actual correctness.
It analyzes how various LLM decoding methods shift probability mass toward different outputs.
The research aims to quantify when the model's predicted probability aligns with a correct answer across different models and methods.

stories.title: Empowering GUI Agents via Autonomous Experience Exploration and Hindsight Experience Utilization for Task Planning
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: The research addresses the weak planning and limited generalization of small, cost-efficient Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) used in GUI agents.
A new method, Planning Experience Exploration and Utilization (PEEU), is introduced to enhance autonomous task planning for web agents.
The goal is to enable small, privacy-preserving MLLMs to effectively decompose complex GUI tasks into executable actions.

stories.title: Empowering GUI Agents via Autonomous Experience Exploration and Hindsight Experience Utilization for Task Planning
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: The research addresses the weak planning and limited generalization of small, cost-efficient MLLMs when used for GUI agent tasks.
A new method, PEEU (Planning Experience Exploration and Utilization), is introduced to enhance autonomous task planning for multimodal web agents.
The work focuses on enabling small, open-source models to effectively decompose complex GUI tasks into executable actions.

stories.title: Beyond Surface Forms: A Comprehensive, Mechanism-Oriented Taxonomy of Indirect Linguistic Encoding for LLM-Based Coded Language Detection
stories.author: Paul Jen-Hwa Hu
bullet_points: The research addresses the problem of indirect linguistic encoding (ILE) used by users to evade social media moderation and surveillance.
It proposes a mechanism-oriented taxonomy to categorize these evasive expressions (like algospeak and euphemisms) based on their underlying encoding operations.
This work is relevant to improving the robustness and accuracy of LLMs in detecting coded or obfuscated language.

stories.title: How Surprising Is Historical Italian to Language Models? Tokenization Tax, Comprehension Tax, and a Simple Mitigation
stories.author: Maria Levchenko
bullet_points: The paper addresses the poorly understood challenge of applying Large Language Models (LLMs) to historical languages, specifically using Italian as a case study.
It introduces a diagnostic framework to decompose historical language difficulty into four specific dimensions, moving beyond a monolithic view.
The framework analyzes distinct costs, including 'tokenization cost' and 'predictive uncertainty (surprisal),' and proposes mitigation strategies.

stories.title: LMs as Task-Specific Knowledge Bases: An Interpretability Analysis
stories.author: Mor Geva
bullet_points: The paper investigates the hypothesis that Large Language Models (LMs) function as knowledge bases.
It analyzes whether LMs maintain the crucial knowledge base property of providing consistent answers for the same fact across different queries.
The findings indicate that LMs encode knowledge in a task-specific manner, suggesting limitations in their 'single source of truth' functionality.

stories.title: Functional doesn't mean correct. That's the biggest risk with AI-generated code.
stories.author: cyclopt_dimitrisk
bullet_points: The article addresses a critical flaw in relying solely on AI-generated code.
It emphasizes that code merely 'running' does not equate to code being 'correct'.
The primary risk highlighted is the existence of hidden failure modes in AI outputs.

stories.title: Cognitive Pong: An Open Source Arena Where AI Agents Compete, Learn, and Train
stories.author: bclonan
bullet_points: Introduces 'Cognitive Pong,' an open-source platform for competitive AI agent training.
The core hypothesis is that structured competition enhances AI reasoning and data quality.
The project aims to generate better training data and ultimately improve AI models through interaction.
